documentation.value: Displays formatted text to the simulator console with an automatic newline.
A format string with specifiers: `%d` decimal, `%h` hex, `%b` binary, `%s` string, `%t` time, `%m` module
Values to substitute into the format string
Displays formatted text to the simulator console without a trailing newline.
A format string with specifiers
Values to substitute into the format string
Monitors and displays signal values whenever any of the listed signals change.
A format string for display
Signals and values to monitor and display
Terminates the simulation. The argument controls the diagnostic message level.
Exit verbosity: `0` no message, `1` simulation time and location, `2` time, location, and statistics
Returns the current simulation time as a 64-bit integer.
Returns a 32-bit signed random integer. An optional seed controls the random sequence.
Optional seed variable for the random number generator

signatures.label: $readmemh(filename: string, memory: reg, start: integer, finish: integer)

documentation.value: Reads hexadecimal values from a file and loads them into a memory array.
The path to the file containing hex data
The memory array to load data into
Optional starting address
Optional ending address

signatures.label: $readmemb(filename: string, memory: reg, start: integer, finish: integer)

documentation.value: Reads binary values from a file and loads them into a memory array.
The path to the file containing binary data
The memory array to load data into
Optional starting address
Optional ending address
Checks that the given expression is true. If false, an assertion failure is reported.
A boolean expression that must be true
Optional action to execute on failure (e.g. `$error`, `$fatal`)
Defines a block of statements that execute once at the beginning of simulation.
Defines a block that re-executes whenever a signal in the sensitivity list changes.
Signals to trigger on: `posedge clk`, `negedge rst`, `*` for all, or combinational signals
Declares a hardware module with optional parameters and port declarations.
The module identifier
Optional parameter declarations for parameterized modules
The module's input, output, and inout port declarations
Declares a function that computes and returns a value in zero simulation time.
The data type of the return value
The function identifier
Input arguments with their types
Declares a task that can contain timing controls and does not return a value.
The task identifier
Input, output, and inout arguments
